mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
This step installs the Google Cloud SDK, which includes the gcloud command-line tool necessary for managing GKE.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

```

> Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
> Reload the shell to access gcloud commands.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure the cluster name matches the one created in the previous step.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the GKE cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step enables monitoring and autoscaling for your GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=us-central1

gcloud services enable monitoring.googleapis.com

```

> Adjust min/max nodes as needed.
> Enables Google Cloud Monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Use --quiet to skip confirmation prompts.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command checks for and removes any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent disks.

```bash

gcloud compute addresses delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Replace with any orphaned resources you may have.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check for any deprecated features before upgrading.
> Verify that all nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 35.185.XXX.XXX:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the cluster with 'gcloud container clusters list' and ensure your network allows access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'The cluster is not ready yet'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster is still initializing.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and check the status again with 'gcloud container clusters describe my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this operation'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the user account lacks the necessary IAM permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure your account has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role or equivalent permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: Quota exceeded for region'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your Google Cloud project has hit its resource quota limits.

**Fix:**
Check your quotas in the Google Cloud Console and request an increase if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for the new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved workload identity.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecation of legacy network policies in favor of the new network policy API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review and update any configurations that rely on deprecated features before upgrading.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, indicating that your GKE cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured before proceeding.
